claimText: The Globally Important Agricultural Heritage Systems (GIAHS) programme was launched at the World Summit for Sustainable Development in 2002.
evidenceExcerpt: Launched at the World Summit for Sustainable Development in 2002, the programme protects family farming and traditional systems.
examUse: Statement-based question on the origin and timeline of the GIAHS programme.
distractorHooks: Launched at the 1992 Earth Summit in Rio
Launched during the Paris Climate Agreement in 2015

claimText: GIAHS protects traditional agricultural systems, family farming, and agrobiodiversity from threats such as climate change, community displacements, and biodiversity loss.
evidenceExcerpt: Protects traditional agricultural systems from climate change, community displacements, and biodiversity loss.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the core objectives and focus areas of GIAHS.
distractorHooks: Focuses primarily on promoting modern, high-yield commercial agriculture
Aims exclusively at wildlife conservation rather than agroecosystems

claimText: India has three recognized GIAHS sites: Saffron Heritage of Kashmir, Koraput Traditional Agriculture in Odisha, and Kuttanad Below Sea Level Farming System in Kerala.
evidenceExcerpt: India has three GIAHS sites: Saffron Heritage of Kashmir, Koraput Traditional Agriculture (Odisha), and Kuttanad Below Sea Level Farming System (Kerala).
examUse: Correct-pair question matching Indian GIAHS sites to their respective states or identifying recognized sites.
distractorHooks: Mismatching states like Koraput in Chhattisgarh or Kuttanad in Tamil Nadu
Including non-GIAHS sites like Darjeeling Tea estates or Kangra Valley agriculture
